author: iPhoneUser_NYC
title: Echo chamber is real and frustrating
body: I've been premium for 4 years. The echo chamber problem is getting worse not better. My Discover Weekly has become completely predictable - within 5 songs I could tell you exactly what genre and era every recommendation will be. The algorithm has stopped taking risks. I remember when Discover Weekly felt like magic. Now it feels like a machine that's too afraid to show me something I might not like.

author: MacUser_Seattle
title: Discover Weekly shows songs I already liked - defeats the purpose
body: Three of my Discover Weekly songs this week were already in my liked songs. TWO more were from artists I follow. That's 5 out of 30 songs that absolutely should not appear in a 'discovery' playlist. Does Spotify check against your existing library before generating Discover Weekly? It clearly doesn't do it well. Basic quality control issue that fundamentally undermines the feature.

author: FreeUser_LA
title: Great for listening, poor for discovering
body: Spotify is undeniably great for listening to music I know I like. The catalog is massive, the app is smooth, audio quality is solid. But I've never actually discovered music through Spotify. Every discovery I've made in the last 2 years has been through YouTube, TikTok, or friends' recommendations. I then go to Spotify to actually listen. Spotify has become the playback platform, not the discovery platform for me. That seems like a missed opportunity.

author: PowerListener_Boston
title: Algorithmic fatigue - same songs for 6 months straight
body: I feel genuinely fatigued by Spotify's algorithm. For 6 months I have heard variations of the same 150 songs. The algorithm has identified my 'safe zone' and refuses to move outside it. What's worse is I've noticed I've become less curious about music in general because Spotify has trained me to expect the same thing every session. This is a harm to my relationship with music and I'm seriously considering cancelling over it.

author: WorkFromHome_User
title: Background work music has completely taken over my taste profile
body: I work from home and keep Spotify on quietly all day. This means lo-fi and ambient music gets the most passive listening time. Now my entire recommendation profile assumes I exclusively love lo-fi and ambient. When I listen actively in the evenings and want something energetic, I have to manually search because recommendations won't give it to me. Passive background listening should have less algorithmic weight than active engaged listening.

author: TeenUser_Miami
title: TikTok songs keep appearing in recommendations I don't want
body: I'm 17 and use Spotify free. The recommendations push a lot of TikTok trending songs that I've never listened to. I prefer indie and alternative but trending viral songs keep appearing in my recommendations even though I skip them every single time. It feels like Spotify is promoting trending music rather than personalizing to my taste. The 'trending' bias in recommendations is really obvious and annoying.

author: OlderUser_64
title: Classic rock fan getting pop recommendations I never asked for
body: I'm 64 years old. I listen to classic rock almost exclusively. Led Zeppelin, The Doors, Hendrix. My recommendations have started including current pop music that I have zero interest in. I have never once clicked on or listened to a pop song but Spotify keeps pushing it at me. I don't understand why. The algorithm seems to think that age demographic overrides personal taste, which is not the right approach. I know what I like and it's not current pop.

author: JazzEnthusiast_Chicago
title: Jazz radio is a travesty - same 20 standards on loop
body: I love jazz and use Spotify's jazz radio features extensively. Or I used to. Now every jazz radio - regardless of the artist I seed it from - plays the same 20-30 jazz standards. Miles Davis, Coltrane, Bill Evans on infinite repeat. There is an enormous catalog of jazz artists from every era and style, but Spotify's jazz radio algorithm seems to know about 40 of them. The genre-specific discovery is particularly bad for niche genres that need better algorithmic support.

author: MinimalistListener
title: Good app but Discover Weekly quality has dropped significantly
body: Simple review. Discover Weekly used to be excellent. Now it's mediocre. That's the summary. The algorithm has become more conservative and the quality of discovery has suffered as a result. Everything else about the app is fine - UI is clean, catalog is massive, offline mode works. But the flagship discovery feature has declined noticeably in quality over the last 2 years.

author: BilingualListenerMX
title: Algorithm doesn't understand bilingual music taste
body: I'm Mexican-American. I listen to roughly 50/50 Spanish and English music. Spotify's algorithm has never successfully bridged these. Spanish music recommendations are almost entirely reggaeton and Latin pop (which I rarely listen to). English recommendations are fine but no crossover artists or Latin-influenced English music ever appears. The algorithm needs to understand that musical connections transcend language barriers.

author: RadioReplacementUser
title: Switched from FM radio expecting better discovery - got worse
body: Moved from FM radio to Spotify specifically for better music discovery 3 years ago. FM radio would play songs I didn't know but often liked. Spotify plays songs I know and sometimes don't even like anymore. The algorithmic recommendation has LESS discovery value than terrestrial FM radio which has existed for 100 years and doesn't know anything about me. That is a failure of product design. The promise of personalized discovery is not being delivered.

author: HipHopHead_ATL
title: Can't escape mainstream hip hop even though I prefer underground
body: I'm deeply into underground hip hop - boom bap, lo-fi rap, jazz rap. Spotify keeps recommending mainstream trap and commercial hip hop that I have no interest in. The algorithm clearly struggles with genre subsets - it knows I like hip hop but can't distinguish underground from mainstream. I've tried to signal this by explicit liking and creating playlists but the recommendations remain stubbornly mainstream. Underground fans need better representation in the algorithm.

author: SleepPlaylistUser
title: Sleep playlist has contaminated my entire recommendation profile
body: I play a 'sleep sounds' or ambient playlist every night to fall asleep. Spotify counts these listening hours but I'm obviously not actively engaging with the music. These passive sleep sessions have made my algorithm think I love ambient/sleep music above all else. Now my Discover Weekly is 80% ambient music. There should be a 'sleep/background mode' where listening doesn't count toward your taste profile.

author: GenuinelyAngry_User
title: Cancelled after 5 years due to recommendation staleness
body: Just cancelled my 5-year Premium subscription. The reason: my Discover Weekly has not shown me a genuinely new discovery in over 8 months. I tracked this. Every single song in my Discover Weekly for the last 8 months has been either: (1) already in my library, (2) by an artist I already follow, or (3) a song I've explicitly heard before. This is not discovery. This is algorithmic failure. Apple Music's recommendation quality is significantly better. Goodbye Spotify.

author: ContentStudent
title: Pretty good overall, wish discovery was stronger
body: Overall happy Spotify user. The app does what it needs to. One area for improvement: the discovery features could be more adventurous. My Discover Weekly is decent but feels like it's playing it very safe. Would love the option to turn on an 'explorer mode' that deliberately pushes outside my comfort zone. Some weeks I want comfort, some weeks I want genuine novelty. The ability to choose would be great.
